products.title: Mangrove
products.body_html:
Mangrove: The Rhizophora Propagule

The Mangrove (Rhizophora mangle), often referred to simply by its genus Rhizophora, is the most common mangrove species kept in the marine aquarium hobby. Famous for its "walking" roots, this salt-tolerant tree is typically acquired as a long, cigar-shaped propagule (seed pod). It is an excellent choice for aquarists looking to create a lagoon biotope or enhance their filtration system naturally.

In the wild, Rhizophora trees form dense coastal forests that protect shorelines. In the home aquarium, they are prized for their aesthetic appeal and their utility as a nutrient export mechanism. As the plant grows, it consumes Nitrates and Phosphates from the water column, helping to outcompete nuisance algae. Unlike macroalgae, mangroves are vascular plants that grow slowly and require their leaves to be exposed to the air.

Successful care of Rhizophora requires understanding its unique biology. It is not fully aquatic; the roots must be submerged in saltwater (or rooted in sand/mud), but the leaves and upper stem must remain above the waterline. If fully submerged, the plant will eventually perish. They are architectural beauties that can eventually develop complex prop roots, adding a dramatic, naturalistic look to open-top tanks or refugiums.

Care & Compatibility:

  • Nutrient Control: This plant is a Phosphate and Nitrate sponge. It incorporates these nutrients into its tissue as it grows. Because it is a slow grower compared to algae like Chaetomorpha, it is best used for maintenance and stability rather than rapid nutrient crashes.
  • Placement: Place the propagule in the sump, overflow box, or directly in the display tank. You can use a floating raft or wedge the pod between rocks. Ensure the growth tip is strictly emergent (in the air).
  • Lighting: Rhizophora requires moderate to strong illumination to thrive. A standard reef light is often sufficient if the plant is positioned correctly, but a dedicated 6,500K freshwater plant light or daylight bulb focused on the leaves will yield the best growth rates.
  • Magnesium Dependency: Mangroves utilize Magnesium to regulate their salt processing. In systems with many mangroves, magnesium levels can drop significantly. Regular testing and dosing are required to prevent the leaves from yellowing.
  • Salt Spray Removal: The plant excretes excess salt through pores in its leaves. This manifests as white crystals. You must mist the leaves with fresh water 2-3 times a week to wash this salt away, or the leaves will burn and drop.
  • Root System: The root system is robust and can damage silicone seals or plastic baffles if allowed to grow unchecked in a tight space. Trimming roots is possible but should be done carefully.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Rhizophora mangle
  • Common Name: Mangrove, Walking Tree
  • Plant Type: Vascular Marine Plant (Halophyte)
  • Origin: Tropical Atlantic / Indo-Pacific
  • Max Size: Can grow into a tree (bonsai techniques required in captivity)
  • Placement: Roots submerged, Leaves in air
  • Lighting: Moderate to High (Daylight spectrum)
  • Supplements: Requires Magnesium
  • Reef Compatibility: Reef Safe (Excellent for refugiums)

products.title: Bucephalandra
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra theia

Buce plants, or bucephalandra, became popular for planted aquariums about a decade ago and are favored by beginner and advanced aquarium aquascapers. The buce plant grows slow, does not need special lighting or CO2 and handles cooler temperatures. Bucephalandra will not tolerate high temperatures so warmer tropical fish tanks that are 80 degrees are not a good fit. Each order comes with one small loose bunch. Look to purchase binder, or just use rubberbands for the short term to attach the buce plant to a rock or driftwood.

  • Scientific Name:  Bucephalandra theia
  • Shipping Size: Small bunch ready to attach to plant on rock or wood subtrate

products.title: African Knifefish
products.body_html:
African Knifefish

Not beautiful, but rare and unusual, the African knifefish has large eyes, barbs, and a long thin light chocolate body. Wild caught in Nigeria they are quite hardy and prefer live or frozen blood worms and similar food. These guys like to school in the wild and if you have three in your fish tank they will always stick together. 

  • Scientific Name: Xenomystus nigri
  • Origin: Nigeria
  • Lifespan: 10 years
  • Max size: 9 inches
  • Food: Very small live feeders, frozen blood worms
  • Shipping Size: Approx. 5 inches

products.title: L-333 King Tiger Pleco
products.body_html:
The L-333 King Tiger Pleco, also called the gold tiger pleco, has a color pattern similar to the L-46 Zebra Pleco, but with a gold background instead of white. Like most plecos, these exotic aquarium fish need some driftwood in the fish tank. 

Most L-333 King Tiger Plecos are tank raised in the trade, but some wild caught species from Brazil are available. AquariumFish.com offers both wild and tank raised fish, depending upon availability.

  • Scientific name: HYPANCISTRUS sp.
  • Origin: Brazil
  • Lifespan: 12 years
  • Max size:  5 inches
  • Food: Live, frozen, flake
  • Shipping Size: 2 inches

products.title: Blood Orange Cyphastrea
products.body_html:
Blood Orange Cyphastrea: The Vibrant Encrusting Gem

The Blood Orange Cyphastrea (Cyphastrea species) is a highly desirable and exceptionally hardy Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral that exhibits an encrusting or plating growth form. This specific strain is revered for its stunning, vivid coloration: a deep, saturated orange or crimson red base that often features contrasting bright red, orange, or sometimes blue polyps.

The Blood Orange Cyphastrea is a fantastic choice for adding color and texture to the shaded or vertical surfaces of reef rockwork, as it tends to encrust rapidly once settled. Its growth form is typically massive and encrusting. The brightness of the orange and red hues is maintained under moderate light intensity and stable, clean water conditions, making it an excellent focal point for the mid-to-lower sections of a reef tank.

This coral is considered Easy to Moderate in care. It is highly tolerant of varying flow conditions and is generally much less demanding than Acropora or high-end Montipora species, making it suitable for beginners looking to venture into SPS corals.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also an opportunistic Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but actively feeds at night.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for supporting growth and polyp extension. The polyps are large enough to consume fine particulate foods. Feed 2-3 times per week with fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Cyphastrea species are known for being non-aggressive and do not extend long, potent sweeper tentacles. However, as an SPS coral, direct contact with aggressive LPS corals (like Euphyllia or Chalices) should be avoided to prevent damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It can be placed almost anywhere, but it thrives best on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate light and moderate to low water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-250 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Cyphastrea sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Blood Orange Cyphastrea
  • Other Common Names: Red Cyphastrea, Blood Cyphastrea, Red Planet Cyphastrea (not to be confused with the Acropora)
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can encrust indefinitely; small colonies grow 2-4 inches per year.
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Easy to Moderate

products.title: Big R Weirdo Chalice
products.body_html:
Big R Weirdo Chalice: The Polychromatic Masterpiece

The Big R Weirdo Chalice is an exceptionally rare and highly valued collector strain of Chalice Coral, typically belonging to the Large Polyp Stony (LPS) family (Echinophyllia or Mycedium). This strain is renowned for its intense polychromatic coloration—a mesmerizing mix that usually includes a deep purple, blue, or red base with multiple contrasting neon yellow, green, and orange corallites (mouths) and streaks. The "Weirdo" name refers to its unpredictable and highly unusual pattern of colors.

The Big R Weirdo Chalice grows in an encrusting and then thick, plating or cup-shaped morphology. Maintaining its intricate, multi-layered color pattern requires precise attention to stable, high-quality water chemistry and placing it under moderate light intensity where its colors can fluoresce without bleaching.

This coral is considered Moderate to Advanced in care. While hardy once established, its value and reliance on perfect conditions to maintain color saturation make it a demanding, high-end showpiece for the dedicated hobbyist.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a vigorous Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but greatly benefits from direct feeding to support its dense tissue and growth.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is strongly recommended for optimal health, growth, and maximum color display. The mouths (corallites) actively feed, extending sweepers at night to capture prey. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Semi-Aggressive to Aggressive. Chalice corals extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night, especially when hunting or defending territory. It is essential to give the Big R Weirdo Chalice at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all other neighboring corals to prevent chemical stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork or on the sand bed where it can encrust and plate outwards. Placement under moderate light (lower section of the tank) is typically best for color retention.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 10-30 ppm, Phosphate 0.01-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (380-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Low to Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Echinophyllia sp. or Mycedium sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Big R Weirdo Chalice
  • Other Common Names: Weirdo Chalice, Polychromatic Chalice
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large plates (10+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Semi-Aggressive (Territorial, uses sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, with proper spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate to Advanced

products.title: Space Invaders Chalice
products.body_html:
Space Invaders Chalice: The Green and Orange Icon

The Space Invaders Chalice is a highly popular and widely recognized strain of Chalice Coral, typically classified within the Large Polyp Stony (LPS) family, often as Echinophyllia or Mycedium. This specific strain is famous for its distinct, high-contrast coloration: a vibrant, electric neon green base or rim that sharply contrasts with prominent orange to reddish-orange corallites (mouths) or eyes, giving it a pixelated, retro video-game appearance.

The Space Invaders Chalice grows in an encrusting and then plating or cup-shaped morphology. Its intense, fluorescent green and orange colors are best maintained under moderate light intensity and stable water chemistry, making it an excellent focal point for the mid-to-lower regions of a reef aquarium.

This coral is considered Moderate in care. It is relatively hardy compared to SPS corals, but it requires consistent parameters and protection from being placed too high in the tank, as high light can cause the colors to fade or the coral to bleach.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a capable Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but greatly benefits from direct feeding to accelerate growth.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is strongly recommended for optimal health and growth. The mouths (corallites) will often extend sweepers at night to capture food. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Semi-Aggressive to Aggressive. Chalice corals extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night, especially when defending territory or feeding. It is essential to give the Space Invaders Chalice at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals to prevent stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork or on the sand bed where it can encrust and plate outwards without shading high-light corals.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 10-30 ppm, Phosphate 0.01-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (380-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Low to Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Echinophyllia sp. or Mycedium sp. (WWC/Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Space Invaders Chalice
  • Other Common Names: WWC Space Invaders Chalice, Green Base Orange Eye Chalice
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large plates (10+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Semi-Aggressive (Territorial, uses sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, with proper spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Solar Flare Chalice
products.body_html:
Solar Flare Chalice: The Yellow and Orange Eruption

The Solar Flare Chalice is a highly sought-after collector strain of Chalice Coral, typically belonging to the Large Polyp Stony (LPS) genus Echinophyllia or Mycedium. This specific strain is famous for its bright, dynamic coloration, featuring a vibrant yellow-gold to orange base that is punctuated by contrasting bright red or sometimes green mouths (corallites) and eyes.

The name "Solar Flare" perfectly captures the brilliant, eruptive nature of its color palette, which strongly glows under actinic (blue) lighting. This coral typically grows in an encrusting and then plating or cup-shaped morphology. Its intense coloration is best maintained under moderate light intensity and stable, moderate nutrient levels, making it a spectacular focal point for the mid-to-lower sections of a reef tank.

The Solar Flare Chalice is considered Moderate in care. While less demanding than high-end SPS corals, it requires consistent water parameters and protection from being placed too high in the tank, where excess light can cause bleaching and dull its characteristic coloration.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a robust Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but benefits significantly from direct feeding.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is strongly recommended to maximize health, growth, and color saturation. The polyps and mouths (corallites) actively feed, extending sweepers at night to capture prey. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Semi-Aggressive to Aggressive. Chalice corals extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night when hunting or defending territory. It is essential to provide the Solar Flare Chalice at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all other neighboring corals to prevent chemical stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork or on the sand bed where it can encrust and plate outwards without shading or stinging high-light corals.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 10-30 ppm, Phosphate 0.01-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (380-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Low to Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Echinophyllia sp. or Mycedium sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Solar Flare Chalice
  • Other Common Names: Yellow Base Red Eye Chalice, Solar Chalice
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large plates (10+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Semi-Aggressive (Territorial, uses sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, with proper spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Raja Rampage Chalice
products.body_html:
Raja Rampage Chalice: The Blazing Red and Green Plate

The Raja Rampage Chalice is a highly sought-after collector strain of Chalice Coral, typically classified within the genus Echinophyllia or Mycedium (LPS). This specific strain is revered for its incredible, high-contrast coloration: a blazing red or fiery orange base that sharply contrasts with neon green to bright yellow mouths (corallites) and eyes.

The name "Raja Rampage" highlights the powerful, almost aggressive use of color, reminiscent of the vibrant chalices found in the Raja Ampat region. This coral grows in an encrusting and then thick, plating or cup-shaped morphology. The red and orange pigmentation is best maintained under moderate light intensity combined with precise water chemistry, making it a spectacular focal point for the lower sections of a reef tank.

This coral is considered Moderate in care, making it suitable for the dedicated intermediate hobbyist. Like all chalices, the Raja Rampage needs consistent water parameters and protection from excessive light, which can cause the colors to dull or the coral to bleach.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a capable Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but benefits greatly from direct feeding.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is strongly recommended for optimal health, growth, and color saturation. The mouths (corallites) will often extend sweepers at night to capture food. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Semi-Aggressive to Aggressive. Chalice corals extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night, especially when actively feeding or competing for space. It is crucial to give the Raja Rampage at least 3-4 inches of clear space from other neighboring corals (especially passive corals like softies) to prevent stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork or on the sand bed where it can encrust and plate outwards without shading high-light corals.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 10-30 ppm, Phosphate 0.01-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (380-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Low to Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Echinophyllia sp. or Mycedium sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Raja Rampage Chalice
  • Other Common Names: Raja Rampage, Red/Orange Chalice
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific, often associated with Raja Ampat)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large plates (10+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Semi-Aggressive (Territorial, uses sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, with proper spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Avatar Chalice
products.body_html:
Avatar Chalice: The Icy Blue and Neon Green Plate

The Avatar Chalice is a highly sought-after collector strain of Chalice Coral, which belongs to the Large Polyp Stony (LPS) family, typically classified under the genus Echinophyllia or Mycedium. This specific strain is revered for its stunning, otherworldly coloration, often featuring an icy blue or deep purple base that contrasts intensely with neon green to fiery red mouths (corallites).

The Avatar Chalice grows in an encrusting and then thick, plating or cup-shaped morphology, which is characteristic of many chalice corals. Its vivid colors intensify significantly under high-actinic (blue) lighting, giving it a bright, bioluminescent look that makes it a spectacular centerpiece for the lower parts of a reef tank.

This coral is considered Moderate in care, making it an excellent choice for the dedicated intermediate hobbyist. While not as demanding as high-end Acropora, the Avatar Chalice still requires consistent parameters and protection from higher light levels.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a capable Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but benefits greatly from direct feeding.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is strongly recommended for health, growth, and vibrant color. The mouths (corallites) will often extend sweepers at night to capture food. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Semi-Aggressive to Aggressive. Chalice corals extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night, especially when food is detected, to compete for space. It is crucial to give the Avatar Chalice at least 3-4 inches of clear space from other neighboring corals to prevent chemical stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork or on the sand bed where it can encrust and plate outwards without shading or stinging other species.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 10-30 ppm, Phosphate 0.01-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (380-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Low to Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Echinophyllia sp. or Mycedium sp. (WWC/Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Avatar Chalice
  • Other Common Names: WWC Avatar Chalice, Blue Base Green Eye Chalice
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large plates (10+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Semi-Aggressive (Territorial, uses sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, with proper spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Tropicana Anacropora
products.body_html:
Tropicana Anacropora: The Orange-Tipped Brancher

The Tropicana Anacropora (Anacropora species) is a beautiful and unique Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral appreciated for its highly contrasting colors and delicate, fuzzy structure. While often lumped in with Acropora or Montipora, Anacropora forms distinct colonies with large, extended polyps. This specific Tropicana strain is known for a base coloration that ranges from pale yellow-green to pinkish-tan, sharply highlighted by vibrant orange or red growth tips.

The Tropicana Anacropora typically grows in a thin, delicate, and multi-branched morphology, often creating a bushy appearance. Its defining feature, common to the genus, is the presence of large, constantly extended polyps, giving the coral a soft, "fuzzy" texture that distinguishes it from the smoother surface of most Acropora.

This coral requires advanced care. It thrives in stable, mature reef tanks with consistent water chemistry. It is sensitive to rough handling and requires careful placement to ensure proper flow without being damaged by aggressive neighbors.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under strong light intensity.
  • Feeding: Due to its large, receptive polyps, the Tropicana Anacropora benefits significantly from supplemental feeding. Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally non-aggressive (passive) toward other corals, as it lacks strong stinging mechanisms. However, its delicate branches can be damaged by aggressive fish or corals with long sweeper tentacles.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned in an area with moderate to high light and moderate, non-damaging, variable water flow that is sufficient to keep detritus from settling.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands moderate to high-PAR lighting (PAR 250-400).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Anacropora sp.
  • Primary Common Name: Tropicana Anacropora
  • Other Common Names: Orange Tip Fuzzy Acro, Tropicana Fuzzy Brancher
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, intricate colonies (6-10+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits heavily from feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 50 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Advanced

products.title: Green Goblin Anacropora
products.body_html:
Green Goblin Anacropora: The Fuzzy Green Brancher

The Green Goblin Anacropora (Anacropora species) is a highly desirable and relatively lesser-known Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral that is gaining popularity in the hobby. It is often mistaken for Acropora or Montipora, but Anacropora forms delicate, fuzzy, and intricate colonies. This specific strain is celebrated for its highly saturated, uniform neon green coloration across its skeletal body and extended polyps.

The Green Goblin typically grows in a thin, delicate, and irregularly branching morphology that can appear bushy or bushy-tabling. A key characteristic of the Anacropora genus is the large, fuzzy polyps that are almost always extended, giving the coral a soft texture. This makes it an attractive visual contrast to other smooth-looking SPS corals.

While considered slightly less demanding than some true Acropora (like the high-end Torts), the Green Goblin Anacropora still requires advanced care. It demands stable parameters, consistent trace element supplementation, and adequate light and flow to maintain its intense green color and prevent tissue recession.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under strong light intensity.
  • Feeding: Due to its large, extended polyps, the Green Goblin is highly receptive to feeding and benefits strongly from it. Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally non-aggressive toward neighbors, lacking the potent stinging apparatus of many Acropora species. However, due to its delicate structure, it should be placed away from corals with long sweeper tentacles (like Euphyllia) and robust fish that might knock it over.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned in an area with moderate light and moderate, variable water flow that is gentle enough not to tear the polyps but strong enough to prevent detritus from settling on the delicate branches.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands moderate to high-PAR lighting (PAR 250-400).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Anacropora sp.
  • Primary Common Name: Green Goblin Anacropora
  • Other Common Names: Fuzzy Green Acro, Green Anacropora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, intricate colonies (6-10+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits heavily from feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 50 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent)
  • Care Level: Advanced

products.title: Twisted Sister Acropora
products.body_html:
Twisted Sister Acropora: The Neon Multi-Color Masterpiece

The Twisted Sister Acropora (Acropora species, widely recognized as a strain of A. tenuis or similar delicate branching forms) is a premium and highly valued Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the collector market. This designer strain is famous for exhibiting a complex and dynamic color combination: typically a blue, purple, or pink base that contrasts with vibrant neon green or sometimes yellow polyps and bright, often iridescent growth tips.

The name "Twisted Sister" is inspired by its multi-layered, chaotic coloration and its tendency to grow in a tight, convoluted, or spiraling branching morphology. Achieving and maintaining its intense polychromatic vibrancy is highly dependent on ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS) and high-intensity, carefully tuned lighting that utilizes both blue and white spectrums.

The Twisted Sister Acropora is strictly designated for the Expert-Only hobbyist. It requires a long-established, highly stable reef system, as it is extremely intolerant of fluctuations in alkalinity and can rapidly lose color or suffer tissue necrosis (RTN/STN) if stressed.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under strong light intensity.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is strongly recommended to support its demanding coloration and growth, especially in ULNS systems. Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as amino acid solutions, cyclops, or commercial coral powders. Nutrient input must be strictly monitored to maintain ULNS status.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but fiercely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it is chemically and physically aggressive toward its neighbors. It requires ample space, ideally 6-8 inches or more of clearance from all other corals to avoid damage from stinging filaments.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned in the middle-to-upper region of the aquarium to maximize light and ensure strong, chaotic water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires zero tolerance for instability. The tank must be mature with ultra-low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.05 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.0), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands very intense, high-PAR lighting (PAR 350-550+) to drive color production.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora sp. (Likely a variant of A. tenuis or similar)
  • Primary Common Name: Twisted Sister Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Multicolored Tenuis Acro, Twisted Acro
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, intricate colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits from plankton/aminos)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (Established, dedicated SPS reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Expert Only

products.title: Miyagi Tort Acropora
products.body_html:
Miyagi Tort Acropora: The Neon Green Standard

The Miyagi Tort Acropora (Acropora species, widely believed to be a stabilized color form of A. tortuosa or A. abrolhosensis) is a foundational and extremely popular Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the collector market. This strain set the benchmark for high-saturation color, featuring an intensely uniform neon or electric green body with bright blue or lavender growth tips.

The name "Tort" (Tortuosa) refers to its characteristic twisting or winding branching morphology. The striking contrast between the electric green body and the sharp blue tips is a result of optimal conditions: high-intensity lighting (heavy in the blue spectrum) and stable, low-nutrient water chemistry.

The Miyagi Tort Acropora is designated for the Advanced to Expert hobbyist. While considered one of the hardier "designer" Acropora, it still demands impeccable water parameters, especially consistent alkalinity, and a mature reef system to prevent browning out or rapid tissue loss (RTN/STN) under stress.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under strong light intensity.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for supporting dense growth and color vibrancy. Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as amino acid solutions, cyclops, or commercial coral powders. Nutrient inputs must be controlled.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but fiercely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it is chemically and physically aggressive toward neighboring corals. It requires ample space, ideally 6 inches or more of clearance from all other corals to prevent stinging.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned in the highest-light region of the aquarium and exposed to strong, turbulent, and chaotic water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires extreme stability. The tank must be mature with low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands very intense, high-PAR lighting (PAR 400-600+).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora sp. (Likely a variant of A. tortuosa or A. abrolhosensis)
  • Primary Common Name: Miyagi Tort Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Miyagi Tort, Neon Green Tortuosa, Green and Blue Acro

  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)

  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Advanced / Expert

Its intense blue or purple hue is dependent on high concentrations of specific pigments, which are fully developed only under ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS) and high-intensity, blue-spectrum LED lighting.

As a demanding Scleractinia, the Cali Tort Acropora is reserved for the Advanced to Expert hobbyist. Rainbow Rommels Acropora: The Multicolored Turret

The Rainbow Rommels Acropora (Acropora species, specifically a stunning variant of A. abrolhosensis or similar tabling/encrusting species) is a highly desirable and exceptionally rare Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the collector market. This strain is celebrated for exhibiting a complex array of colors simultaneously, often including a base of metallic blue or purple, with yellow-green body tissue, and bright red or pink growth tips. The vivid combination creates a true "rainbow" effect under ideal lighting.

The Rainbow Rommels typically grows in a low, dense tabling or plating morphology with short, clustered branches (turrets). Achieving and maintaining its polychromatic intensity requires meticulous water management, focusing on ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS) and the consistent delivery of minor and trace elements necessary for full color development.

Due to its high sensitivity and reliance on precise environmental control, the Rainbow Rommels Acropora is strictly designated for the Expert-Only hobbyist who can provide an impeccably stable and mature reef environment.

Diet & Compatibility:

Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as amino acid solutions, cyclops, or commercial coral powders. Feeding must be balanced to avoid raising nutrient levels.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but fiercely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it is chemically and physically aggressive. Due to its plating growth, care must be taken that it does not shade or sting corals placed below it. It requires ample space, ideally 6 inches or more of clearance from all neighboring corals.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned in the middle-to-upper region of the aquarium to receive maximum light and strong, variable water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires zero tolerance for instability. The tank must be mature with ultra-low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.05 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.0), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands very intense, high-PAR lighting (PAR 350-550+).

products.body_html:
Purple Planet Acropora: The Deep Purple Plater

The Purple Planet Acropora (Acropora species, widely recognized as a strain of A. efflorescens or similar plating/encrusting forms) is an extremely famous and highly coveted Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the reef aquarium hobby. This classic collector strain is celebrated for its deep, saturated metallic purple to eggplant-colored base which beautifully contrasts with striking neon green or sometimes yellow polyps.

The Purple Planet typically grows in a low, encrusting and then thick, plating or tabling morphology. Achieving and maintaining its intense purple coloration is a true test of an aquarist's skill, as the color relies on specific non-fluorescent pigmentation driven by ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS) and high-intensity, blue-spectrum lighting.

The Purple Planet Acropora is firmly in the Expert-Only category. It requires a highly mature, stable reef system with impeccable water chemistry. It is highly intolerant of alkalinity swings and nutrient spikes, which will quickly cause the color to brown or lead to rapid tissue loss (RTN/STN).

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under strong light intensity.

Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as amino acid solutions, cyclops, or commercial coral powders.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but intensely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it is chemically and physically aggressive toward its neighbors. Due to its plating growth, it can quickly shade out corals below it. It requires ample space, ideally 6 inches or more of clearance from all other corals.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned in the middle-to-upper region of the aquarium to receive maximum light and strong water flow across its plate to prevent detritus accumulation.
  • Water Requirements: Requires zero tolerance for instability. The tank must be mature with ultra-low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.05 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-8.5), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands very intense, high-PAR lighting (PAR 350-550+).

This designer strain is celebrated for its incredible contrast: a vibrant, dense electric purple or lilac-colored base and branches that are tipped with striking neon green or yellow growth tips and polyps.

The intense, saturated purple coloration of the Lilac Attack is dependent on specific conditions, usually requiring ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS) and high-intensity, blue-spectrum lighting. products.body_html:
Indo Queen Acropora: The Deep Water Beauty

The Indo Queen Acropora (Acropora species, often believed to be a deeper water variant of A. tenuis or a similar branching species) is a highly desirable and relatively rare strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. This coral is admired for its beautiful and complex color pattern, typically featuring a dark blue or purple base with striking bright green or neon yellow polyps that contrast vividly against the skeletal body.

Unlike some shallower water Acropora that grow densely, the Indo Queen often exhibits a more open, delicately branching structure, characteristic of corals found in lower-light environments. However, to maintain its intense color saturation in the aquarium, it still requires controlled, high-quality lighting, often emphasizing the blue spectrum.

Like all collector Acropora strains, the Indo Queen demands advanced to expert care. It requires pristine, stable water chemistry, especially consistent alkalinity and ultra-low nutrient levels (ULNS), to prevent the dark base from fading or the coral from browning (browning out).

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It hosts symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, which provide the vast majority of its energy needs through light absorption.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is strongly recommended to support its nutritional needs and growth. Target feed the coral polyps 2-3 times per week with fine, high-quality particulate foods, such as amino acid solutions, cyclops, or commercial coral powders. Nutrient control (ULNS) must be maintained even while feeding.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but intensely Territorial. As a true Scleractinia, it is highly aggressive toward adjacent corals. It requires 6 inches or more of clearance from all other corals to prevent chemical or physical warfare via stinging cells.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. Placement should be in the mid-to-upper tank area under controlled lighting and moderate to strong, turbulent water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires expert-level water stability and ultra-low nutrient levels. Any fluctuations in alkalinity (dKH) can cause rapid tissue loss (RTN/STN). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.0), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands intense, high-quality lighting (PAR 300-450+) and strong water flow.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora sp. (Likely a variant of A. tenuis or similar)
  • Primary Common Name: Indo Queen Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Blue Base Green Tip Acro, Deepwater Blue Acropora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from Indonesia/Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, open colonies (8-15+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits from feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (Established, dedicated SPS reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Advanced / Expert

products.title: Ice Cap Acropora
products.body_html:
Ice Cap Acropora: The Blue Tipped Showpiece

The Ice Cap Acropora (Acropora millepora) is one of the most recognized and sought-after Small Polyp Stony (SPS) corals, valued for its compact growth structure and stunning coloration. This specific strain of A. millepora is characterized by a dense, bottlebrush or tabling morphology with thick branches covered in fuzzy, extended polyps, giving it a soft appearance despite its hard, calcareous skeleton.

The name "Ice Cap" is derived from its signature look: a vibrant, uniform body color—typically a rich emerald green or deep teal—that contrasts dramatically with its intense, electric blue or white growth tips. This high-contrast coloration makes it a centerpiece in any dedicated SPS reef tank. The color intensity is heavily dependent on the quality and spectrum of lighting provided.

While considered slightly hardier than some other Acropora species, the Ice Cap Acropora still requires advanced care, demanding exceptional water stability, intense light, and rigorous adherence to calcium, alkalinity, and magnesium supplementation to support its dense skeleton.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies almost entirely on the energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, thriving under high light intensity.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster and denser growth. Target feed the coral polyps 2-3 times per week with very fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or high-quality amino acid solutions and commercial coral powders. A. millepora polyps are known to extend well, making them receptive to feeding.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but fiercely Territorial. Like all Acropora, it is a hard coral and is aggressive toward neighboring corals. It requires 6 inches or more of clearance from other corals (SPS and LPS) to prevent damage from stinging cells or chemical warfare.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. Place it in the middle to upper half of the tank to maximize light and ensure it receives turbulent flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires advanced water stability. The tank must be mature with low nutrients (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands intense, high-PAR lighting (PAR 300-500+) and strong, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup within the dense branches.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora millepora (Specific strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Ice Cap Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Ice Cap Milly, Blue Tip Millepora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size:** Can grow into large, dense colonies (6-12+ inches)

Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits from feeding)Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (Established, dedicated SPS reef)Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.Care Level: Advanced

products.title: Red Dragon Acropora
products.body_html:
Red Dragon Acropora: The Deep Red Icon

The Red Dragon Acropora (Acropora species, widely recognized as a strain of Acropora formosa or A. caroliniana) is arguably the most famous and highly coveted Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the reef aquarium hobby. This coral is renowned for its unmatched deep red or burgundy coloration on its branches and polyps, combined with brilliant white or pale pink growth tips, creating a fiery, almost metallic appearance.

The Red Dragon grows in a delicate, open, and slightly straggly branching morphology, unlike the denser structures of many other Acropora strains. Its dramatic color is due to a high concentration of non-fluorescent red pigmentation (chromoprotein), which requires a specific set of environmental factors—most notably ultra-low nutrient levels and high-intensity lighting—to fully develop.

This is an Expert-Only coral. It is extremely sensitive to changes in water chemistry and is often used as a canary in the coal mine for water quality, as the deep red color will quickly fade to brown if nutrients (nitrate/phosphate) rise above trace levels or if alkalinity fluctuates.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It relies heavily on the energy produced by its symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, which requires intense light.
  • Feeding: While light is critical, supplemental feeding is recommended, especially for frag growth. Target feed the coral polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine planktonic foods, such as amino acid solutions, coral powders, or fine rotifers. Nutrient control is paramount, so feeding must be balanced with filtration.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but extremely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it is a hard coral and is chemically or physically aggressive toward its neighbors. It requires at least 6-8 inches of clearance from all other corals to prevent tissue loss from stinging or chemical aggression.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It demands placement in the upper half of the aquarium under the strongest lighting and flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires zero tolerance for instability. The tank must be biologically mature with ultra-low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.05 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-8.5), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands very intense, high-quality lighting (PAR 400-600+) and very strong, laminar water flow to prevent dead spots and enhance color.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora sp. (Likely a variant of A. formosa)
  • Primary Common Name: Red Dragon Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Red Dragon Acro, Deep Red Acropora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, intricate colonies (8-15+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits from plankton/aminos)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (Established, dedicated SPS reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Expert Only

products.title: Bubble Yum Acropora
products.body_html:
Bubble Yum Acropora: The Pink and Green Delight

The Bubble Yum Acropora (Acropora species, likely a variety of A. microclados or A. loripes) is a highly sought-after and collectible Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral in the reef aquarium hobby. This coral is a prized specimen known for its unique and stunning dual coloration: a vibrant neon green body that beautifully contrasts with bright pink to magenta growth tips and occasionally pink polyps, reminiscent of bubblegum.

Like many designer Acropora strains, the Bubble Yum grows in a dense, branching or bottlebrush morphology, demanding specific environmental conditions to display its signature colors. This coral is considered Expert Only due to its extreme sensitivity. It is a true Scleractinia (reef-building) coral that requires pristine, unwavering water chemistry, high calcium/alkalinity levels, intense lighting, and robust, turbulent flow.

Maintaining the intense pink and green coloration requires precise control over lighting spectrum (often demanding higher blue spectrum light) and nutrients, making it a true showpiece coral for the dedicated, advanced hobbyist.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It hosts symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, which provide the vast majority of its energy needs through high light absorption.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly recommended to encourage optimal growth and color saturation. Target feed the polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial amino acid solutions and coral powders.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but extremely Territorial. Like all Acropora species, it may extend stinging mesenterial filaments or sweeper tentacles. It requires at least 6-8 inches of distance from all other corals (both SPS and LPS), as contact will result in tissue loss for both parties.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be placed in the upper portion of the tank to maximize light and flow exposure, away from competitors.
  • Water Requirements: Requires expert-level water stability; any fluctuation in alkalinity (dKH) can cause immediate stress. Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands intense lighting (PAR 350-550+) and very strong, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora sp. (Likely A. microclados or A. loripes variant)
  • Primary Common Name: Bubble Yum Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Bubblegum Acro, Pink Tip Green Acro, Pink & Green Acropora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large colonies (6-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits heavily from feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (Established, dedicated SPS reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Expert

products.title: Bali Green Slimer Acropora
products.body_html:
Bali Green Slimer Acropora: The Fast-Growing SPS

The Bali Green Slimer Acropora (Acropora yongei) is one of the most popular and iconic Small Polyp Stony (SPS) corals in the reef aquarium hobby. Prized for its exceptionally fast growth rate and striking, consistent coloration, this coral grows in a dense, branching or staghorn morphology. It is a highly valued coral, as it is one of the easiest Acropora species to frag and propagate.

The Green Slimer is characterized by its vibrant neon green color across its body and polyps, and it often has purple or blue growth tips when thriving under intense light. Its name "Slimer" comes from the copious amounts of mucus it produces when stressed or handled, a defense mechanism common among Acropora species. This coral demands expert care, requiring immaculate and highly stable water chemistry, intense light, and turbulent water flow.

As a true Scleractinia (reef-building) coral, the Bali Green Slimer is a showpiece, but it is highly sensitive to fluctuations in alkalinity and is prone to various types of coral tissue necrosis (STN/RTN) if stressed.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic. It hosts symbiotic zooxanthellae algae, which provide the vast majority of its energy needs through light.
  • Feeding: While photosynthesis is key, supplemental feeding is highly beneficial due to its fast growth. Target feed the coral polyps 2-3 times per week with high-quality, fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders. Shut off powerheads briefly during target feeding to allow the food to settle.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but requires careful placement. Like most Acropora, it can extend stinging mesenterial filaments to defend territory. It needs 6 inches or more of separation from soft corals and Large Polyp Stony (LPS) corals, as it will likely be chemically or physically damaged by them.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Highly Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It thrives best in a dedicated SPS-dominant system.
  • Water Requirements: Requires expert-level water stability with unwavering parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.0), Calcium (420-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1400 ppm). Demands intense lighting (PAR 300-550+) and very strong, turbulent water flow to keep tissue healthy and prevent dead spots.

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Acropora yongei (often incorrectly sold as Acropora muricata)
  • Primary Common Name: Bali Green Slimer Acropora
  • Other Common Names: Green Slimer Acro, Green Staghorn Acropora, Acropora yongei
  • Origin: Indo-Pacific (Bali, Fiji, Australia, Indonesia)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large colonies (12+ inches in captivity)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic (Zooxanthellate; benefits from feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 75 Gallons (established, stable reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires expert-level spacing and stability.
  • Care Level: Advanced / Expert

products.title: Mystic Sunset Montipora
products.body_html:
Mystic Sunset Montipora: The Glowing Orange and Yellow Plate

The Mystic Sunset Montipora (Montipora species) is a highly fluorescent and immensely popular collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are foundational corals in the reef hobby, recognized for their hardiness and attractive growth forms.

This specific strain is named "Mystic Sunset" for its beautiful, gradient coloration reminiscent of a vibrant evening sky. It typically features a base tissue that ranges from bright sunset orange to deep red or reddish-brown. The surface is densely dotted with numerous, brightly colored polyps in neon yellow or electric green. The contrast between the warm base and the neon polyps gives the coral an intense, shimmering glow.

The Mystic Sunset Montipora commonly grows in an encrusting or thinly plating morphology, quickly forming broad, overlapping layers. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is generally hardier than Acropora but requires consistent water chemistry, high light, and ample water flow to maintain its rapid growth and brilliant color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust polyp extension. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles. However, as a fast-encrusting/plating coral, it can quickly grow over and shade out adjacent, slower-growing corals. Proper spacing is essential to prevent territorial disputes via shading.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surfaces.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Mystic Sunset Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Sunset Montipora, Orange Base Yellow Polyp Monti
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large encrusting colonies/plates (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and competitive via shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Megatron Montipora
products.body_html:
Megatron Montipora: The Intense Blue and Red Destroyer

The Megatron Montipora (Montipora species) is an elite, highly sought-after collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are foundational corals in the reef hobby, known for their rapid growth and varied morphology.

This specific strain is named "Megatron" for its dark, powerful, and highly contrasting coloration, often featuring colors associated with the Transformers villain. It typically exhibits a dominant base tissue of deep electric blue, violet, or dark purple. This base is dramatically contrasted by numerous, densely packed polyps in bright neon red, pink, or orange. The combination creates an intense, vibrant fluorescence that makes it a spectacular centerpiece.

The Megatron Montipora commonly grows in an encrusting or plating morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. While hardier than Acropora, it requires consistent, pristine water chemistry, high light, and strong water flow to achieve and maintain its remarkable color saturation and rapid growth.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust health. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its plating growth can quickly expand and aggressively shade out lower-lying or slower-growing corals. Strategic placement is essential to prevent it from dominating the aquascape.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surfaces and encourage vibrant coloration.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Consistency in alkalinity is crucial. Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Megatron Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Megatron Plating Monti, Blue/Red Polychromatic Monti
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large encrusting colonies/plates (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but aggressively competitive via shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Mai Tai Montipora Setosa
products.body_html:
Mai Tai Montipora Setosa: The Tropical Orange Highlighter

The Mai Tai Montipora Setosa (Montipora setosa species) is an incredibly vibrant and sought-after collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora setosa is a uniquely fuzzy, plating-to-encrusting species known for its strong fluorescence and ease of care relative to other SPS.

This specific strain is named "Mai Tai" for its brilliant, tropical, cocktail-inspired coloration. It features an intense, highly saturated hot orange or reddish-orange base tissue. This base is dramatically highlighted by numerous, densely packed, contrasting polyps in bright neon yellow or yellowish-green. The fine, fuzzy polyps give the coral a soft texture and an overall intense, glowing appearance.

The Mai Tai Montipora Setosa grows in a thickly encrusting, tiered, or occasionally plating morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral and is highly durable. It requires consistent water chemistry, high light, and ample water flow to maintain its rapid growth and maximum color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust polyp extension. Its small, fuzzy polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora setosa lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its growth form is aggressive in terms of territory. It can quickly encrust and overgrow adjacent corals, competing effectively for space. Proper placement is essential.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (300-450 PAR), which is crucial for maintaining the deep orange color, and strong, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup and encourage even growth.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora setosa (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Mai Tai Montipora Setosa
  • Other Common Names: Mai Tai Monti, Hot Orange Setosa
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Forms thick encrusting plates (8+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but aggressively encrusting)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, hardy SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Tubbs Stellata Montipora
products.body_html:
Tubbs Stellata Montipora: The Classic Deep Blue Encruster

The Tubbs Stellata Montipora (Montipora stellata or similar encrusting Montipora species) is an iconic and foundational collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are highly favored for their hardiness and ability to add color and texture to the aquascape.

This specific strain, often associated with the renowned coral vendor "Tubbs," is famous for its saturated, uniform deep blue or dark purple base tissue. Unlike some Montipora that rely on contrasting polyp colors, the beauty of the Tubbs Stellata lies in the intensity and depth of its tissue color, which fluoresces brilliantly under blue-spectrum lighting. Its polyps are typically small and slightly lighter than the base.

The Tubbs Stellata Montipora grows in a thickly encrusting or massive plating morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is one of the hardier deep-water style Montipora and requires consistent water chemistry, but its best color is achieved under moderate light and ample flow, making it ideal for the middle section of the reef.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for supporting robust tissue growth and deeper color saturation. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but as a fast-encrusting coral, it can grow quickly and compete for space by growing over rockwork and slower-moving neighbors. Proper spacing is necessary.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-level rockwork where it receives Moderate PAR lighting (150-300 PAR), which helps maintain the deep blue coloration, and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surface.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora stellata (or similar encrusting sp.)
  • Primary Common Name: Tubbs Stellata Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Tubbs Blue Monti, Deep Blue Montipora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large encrusting colonies/plates (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and competitive via shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Kung Pao Montipora
products.body_html:
Kung Pao Montipora: The Fiery Red and Yellow Encruster

The Kung Pao Montipora (Montipora species) is a vibrant and highly fluorescent collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are foundational corals in the reef hobby, known for their excellent growth rate and diverse forms.

This specific strain is named "Kung Pao" for its intense, hot coloration that is reminiscent of a spicy pepper dish. It typically features a base tissue that ranges from deep fiery red, brilliant orange, or reddish-brown. This base is dramatically contrasted by numerous, often sparse, polyps in bright neon yellow or chartreuse green. The combination of warm, dense base color and contrasting bright polyps makes it a visually striking addition.

The Kung Pao Montipora commonly grows in an encrusting or thinly plating morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is generally hardier than Acropora but requires consistent water chemistry, high light, and ample water flow to maintain its rapid growth and brilliant color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.

  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles. However, as a fast-encrusting coral, it can quickly grow over and shade out adjacent, slower-growing corals. Proper spacing is crucial to prevent territorial disputes via shading.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surfaces.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)

  • Other Common Names: Kung Pao Plating Monti, Red Base Yellow Polyp Monti
