products.title: Fairy Tale Montipora
products.body_html:
Fairy Tale Montipora: The Pastel Multi-Color Plater

The Fairy Tale Montipora (Montipora species) is a highly delicate and beautiful collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are a diverse group known for their varied growth forms and bright coloration.

This specific strain is named "Fairy Tale" for its soft, ethereal, and complex pastel colors. It often exhibits a blend of hues, typically featuring a pale purple or lavender base tissue that subtly transitions to light blue or pink near the growing edges. The surface is covered by numerous, contrasting polyps in bright neon green or occasionally yellow. The overall effect is soft, iridescent, and highly fluorescent.

The Fairy Tale Montipora typically grows in a thinly plating, scrolling, or encrusting morphology. It is considered a Moderate to Advanced care SPS coral. While Montipora are generally hardy, the delicate pastel coloration of this particular strain requires more rigorous stability, high light, and ample flow to prevent browning or fading.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for supporting rapid growth and maintaining its light, vibrant color. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its plating growth can quickly shade out lower-lying or slower-growing corals. Strategic placement away from aggressive LPS or shading corals is critical.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup and ensure even growth.
  • Water Requirements: Requires extreme stability. The tank must be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Consistency in alkalinity is paramount. Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Fairy Tale Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Fairy Tale Plating Monti, Pastel Blue/Purple Monti
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large plates/colonies (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but capable of shading neighbors)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate to Advanced

products.title: Fruity Pebbles Montipora
products.body_html:
Fruity Pebbles Montipora: The Multicolored Encruster

The Fruity Pebbles Montipora (Montipora species) is a dazzling and immensely popular collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are foundational corals in the reef hobby, known for their excellent growth rate and diverse forms.

This specific strain is named "Fruity Pebbles" because its vivid, polychromatic appearance resembles the multicolored breakfast cereal. It typically features a base tissue that can range from pale green, tan, or light purple but is densely covered with numerous, brightly colored polyps in neon green, electric blue, bright pink, red, and sometimes yellow. This random splatter of highly contrasting colors makes it a unique and eye-catching showpiece.

The Fruity Pebbles Montipora commonly grows in an encrusting or thinly plating morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is generally hardier than Acropora but requires consistent water chemistry, high light, and ample water flow to maintain its rapid growth and brilliant color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust polyp extension. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles. However, as a fast-encrusting coral, it can quickly grow over and shade out adjacent, slower-growing corals. Proper spacing is important.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surfaces.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Fruity Pebbles Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Fruity Pebbles Plating Monti, Polychromatic Monti
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large encrusting colonies/plates (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and competitive via shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Superman Montipora Digitata
products.body_html:
Superman Montipora Digitata: The Red, Blue, and Yellow Hero

The Superman Montipora Digitata (Montipora digitata species) is an iconic and exceptionally popular collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora digitata is one of the hardiest and fastest-growing branching Montipora varieties, known for its rigid, finger-like structure.

This specific strain is named "Superman" for its resemblance to the superhero's classic costume colors: a vibrant deep royal blue or violet base tissue is accented by bright neon red or pink polyps and often includes patches or rims of yellow or orange on the growth tips or base. This triple-color contrast is dramatic and highly fluorescent under blue-spectrum lighting.

The Superman Montipora Digitata grows in a dense, columnar, or branching morphology, forming thickets of tapered branches. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is forgiving of minor parameter fluctuations compared to Acropora, but it demands consistent water chemistry, high light, and strong water flow to maintain its health and signature tri-color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for accelerating growth and enhancing color saturation. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. It lacks long sweeper tentacles, but due to its quick and dense branching growth, it can easily shade out slower-growing corals placed beneath it. Ensure it has ample vertical and horizontal clearance.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (300-450 PAR) and strong, turbulent water flow to support its high metabolic rate and prevent stagnant water within the colony's structure.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora digitata (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Superman Montipora Digitata
  • Other Common Names: Superman Monti, Blue/Red/Yellow Digitata, Tri-Color Branching Montipora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large, dense thickets (8+ inches tall)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, hardy branching SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Red Montipora Digitata
products.body_html:
Red Montipora Digitata: The Scarlet Branching Coral

The Red Montipora Digitata (Montipora digitata species) is a classic and reliable strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora digitata is one of the most robust and fast-growing branching Montipora varieties, popular for its distinctive finger-like growth structure.

This specific strain is prized for its uniform, saturated deep red or scarlet base tissue. The polyps often match the base color or may be slightly lighter red or pink, contributing to a rich, dense crimson appearance. The solid red color is highly visible and adds a dramatic tone to the reef environment.

The Red Montipora Digitata grows in a dense, columnar, or branching morphology, quickly forming thickets of tapered branches. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is generally forgiving regarding minor parameter fluctuations compared to Acropora but requires consistent alkalinity, high light, and strong flow to maintain its rapid, healthy growth and intense red color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for accelerating growth and ensuring the deepest red coloring. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. It lacks long sweeper tentacles, but due to its fast and dense branching growth, it can easily shade out slower-growing corals placed beneath it. Ensure it has ample vertical and horizontal clearance to grow without damaging neighbors.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (300-450 PAR) and strong, turbulent water flow to support its high metabolic rate and prevent stagnant water within the colony's structure.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora digitata (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Red Montipora Digitata
  • Other Common Names: Red Monti, Scarlet Digitata, Cherry Red Branching Montipora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large, dense thickets (8+ inches tall)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)

  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: German Blue Montipora Digitata
products.body_html:
German Blue Montipora Digitata: The Deep Blue Branching Coral

The German Blue Montipora Digitata (Montipora digitata species) is one of the most classic and sought-after collector strains of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora digitata is renowned for being a hardy, relatively fast-growing branching variety, perfect for adding vertical structure to the reef.

This specific strain is prized for its saturated, deep, and uniform electric blue or royal blue base tissue. While the polyps may be slightly lighter blue or white, the overall impression is a vibrant, unmistakable blue that fluoresces strongly under actinic (blue) lighting. It is one of the few corals that maintain such a deep blue coloration in captivity.

The German Blue Montipora Digitata grows in a dense, columnar, or branching morphology, forming thick, finger-like branches. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is forgiving regarding minor parameter swings compared to Acropora, but it requires consistent chemistry, high light, and strong water flow to maintain its growth rate and deep blue color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for accelerating growth and enhancing color. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. It lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its branching growth can quickly shade out and potentially harm slower-growing corals placed beneath it. Place it with ample vertical and horizontal clearance.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (300-450 PAR) and strong, turbulent water flow to support its high metabolic rate and prevent stagnant water within the colony's structure.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora digitata (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: German Blue Montipora Digitata
  • Other Common Names: German Blue Monti, Blue Digitata, Blue Branching Montipora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large, dense thickets (8+ inches tall)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, hardy branching SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Bubblegum Montipora Digitata
products.body_html:
Bubblegum Montipora Digitata: The Bright Pink Branching Coral

The Bubblegum Montipora Digitata (Montipora digitata species) is a highly distinctive and vibrant collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora digitata is one of the hardiest branching Montipora varieties, known for its rapid, dense, finger-like growth structure.

This specific strain is named "Bubblegum" for its unmistakable, intense pink to magenta base tissue. This bright base is often contrasted by vibrant neon green, chartreuse, or sometimes yellow polyps that extend prominently from the branches. The combination of intense pink and neon green creates a spectacular, fluorescent effect, making it a standout in any reef.

The Bubblegum Montipora Digitata grows in a dense, columnar, or branching morphology, forming thickets of tapered, finger-like branches. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. It is generally very forgiving compared to Acropora but requires consistent parameters, high light, and strong flow to maintain its dense branching structure and vivid coloration.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for accelerating growth and enhancing color saturation. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. It does not possess long sweeper tentacles, but due to its fast and dense branching growth, it can easily shade out slower-growing corals placed below it. It should be given vertical clearance.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (300-450 PAR) and strong, turbulent water flow to support its high metabolic rate and prevent stagnant areas within the colony.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora digitata (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Bubblegum Montipora Digitata
  • Other Common Names: Bubblegum Monti, Pink Digitata, Branching Montipora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large, dense thickets (8+ inches tall)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but fast-growing and shading)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)

  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, hardy branching SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Beach Bum Montipora
products.body_html:
Beach Bum Montipora: The Pastel Yellow and Pink Plater

The Beach Bum Montipora (Montipora species) is a beautiful and highly sought-after collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are well-regarded for their ease of care compared to Acropora and their distinctive growth habits.

This specific strain is named "Beach Bum" for its attractive, warm, and sun-kissed pastel coloration. It typically features a pale yellow, creamy pink, or peach base tissue which is heavily contrasted by numerous bright pink or reddish-orange polyps. The overall effect is soft yet vibrant, standing out well in a colorful mixed reef.

The Beach Bum Montipora typically grows in a thinly plating, scrolling, or encrusting morphology. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral. While hardy, it requires consistent parameters, high light, and ample flow to maintain its rapid growth rate and stunning color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust color. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its plating growth can expand rapidly, potentially shading out lower-lying or slower-growing corals. Strategic placement is key to prevent it from dominating the aquascape.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its surfaces and encourage plating growth.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Beach Bum Montipora
  • Other Common Names: Pastel Plating Monti, Yellow Base Pink Polyp Monti
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can form large plates/colonies (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but capable of shading neighbors)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established SPS or Mixed Reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive SPS option)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Afterburner Montipora
products.body_html:
Afterburner Montipora: The Fiery Orange and Red Masterpiece

The Afterburner Montipora (Montipora species) is a highly desirable and exceptionally fluorescent collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral. Montipora species are foundational corals in the reef hobby, known for their varied growth forms and bright colors.

This specific strain is named "Afterburner" for its intense, fiery coloration that mimics a jet engine's exhaust glow. It typically exhibits a dazzling hot orange or deep red base tissue, which is heavily contrasted by bright neon yellow or chartreuse polyps and often includes fluorescent green highlights along the growth edge. This polychromatic combination makes it a spectacular focal point.

The Afterburner Montipora typically grows in a thinly plating or scrolling morphology, though captive fragments may initially encrust. It is considered a Moderate care SPS coral, being hardier than most Acropora but requiring consistent parameters, high light, and ample flow to maintain its rapid growth rate and intense color saturation.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also an opportunistic Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust color. Its small polyps readily capture fine particulate foods. Target feed 2-3 times per week with very fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Montipora lacks powerful sweeper tentacles, but its plating growth can quickly shade out lower-lying or slower-growing corals. Care must be taken to place it where its shadow will not kill other species.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-high rockwork where it receives High PAR lighting (250-450 PAR) and strong, non-laminar, turbulent water flow to prevent detritus buildup on its plating surfaces.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Montipora sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Afterburner Montipora

  • Max Size: Can form large plates/colonies (12+ inches in diameter)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but capable of shading neighbors)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)

products.title: Mr. Sparkle Leptoseris
products.body_html:
Mr. Sparkle Leptoseris: The Metallic Gold and Green Encruster

The Mr. Sparkle Leptoseris (Leptoseris species) is a dazzling and highly valued collector strain of Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral known for its brilliant, metallic shimmer. Leptoseris species are famous for their strong fluorescence and their ability to thrive in shaded or deeper parts of the reef.

This specific strain is named "Mr. Sparkle" due to its intense, iridescent glow, featuring a metallic gold, chartreuse, or yellowish-green base that encrusts the rockwork. This base is punctuated by prominent corallites (polyps) in a contrasting neon green or deep blue. The coral often displays strong, directional fluorescence, appearing to "sparkle" when viewed under blue-spectrum lighting.

The Mr. Sparkle Leptoseris grows in a thin, encrusting, and plating morphology. It is considered a relatively Hardy SPS coral, especially regarding light demands, as it prefers lower PAR levels than Acropora or Montipora. This makes it ideal for adding color to the bottom or shaded areas of an established reef tank.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also an opportunistic Carnivore. Its unique, specialized zooxanthellae allow it to perform photosynthesis efficiently even in low light.
  • Feeding: Supplemental feeding is recommended to support rapid growth and high coloration. Its polyps are small but will extend at night. Target feed 2-3 times per week with fine particulate foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Leptoseris is non-aggressive and lacks potent sweeper tentacles. However, because it is an encrusting coral that prefers lower light, it must be protected from aggressive LPS corals (like Chalices or Galaxea) that extend sweepers and from faster-growing, shading corals like softies or large Montipora caps.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on lower rockwork, vertical faces, or shaded areas where it receives Low to Moderate PAR lighting (50-150 PAR) and moderate, non-turbulent water flow to keep its surface clean.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Leptoseris sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Mr. Sparkle Leptoseris
  • Other Common Names: Metallic Gold Leptoseris, Deepwater SPS
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific, often deeper waters)
  • Max Size: Can encrust indefinitely; forms large plates.
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent for lower light zones)
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Zen Garden Micro Goni
products.body_html:
Zen Garden Micro Goni: The Serene Polychromatic Flower Coral

The Zen Garden Micro Goni is an exceptionally rare and highly valued collector strain of Micro Goniopora (Goniopora species with very small, tight polyps), a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Commonly known as "Flower Pot Corals," Goniopora species are famous for their long, delicate, daisy-like polyps that sway in the current.

This specific "Zen Garden" strain is revered for its serene, complex, and highly sought-after polychromatic coloration. It typically features a base of deep lavender, pink, or metallic purple that contrasts with bright neon green or yellow oral discs (mouths) and pale, often pastel-colored tips on the tiny polyps. Its tight polyp structure makes it visually distinct from the large-polyp Goniopora.

The Zen Garden Micro Goni grows in a massive, encrusting, or hemispherical morphology. Due to the delicate nature of its polyps and its high reliance on specific trace elements and clean feeding, this coral is designated for the Advanced to Expert hobbyist. It requires a mature, highly stable system to thrive.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is Photosynthetic but relies heavily on external feeding. Micro Goniopora species require significant nutritional input, as they are not as efficient at photosynthesis as many other corals.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is crucial for long-term survival and health. The micro polyps readily consume very fine particulate matter. Feed 3-5 times per week with highly concentrated, fine foods, such as Goniopora-specific powders, amino acid solutions, rotifers, and ultra-fine cyclops.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but generally Passive. It does not extend potent sweeper tentacles, but its polyps can extend far. It should be given space, not for aggression, but to prevent other aggressive corals (like Euphyllia or Galaxea) from stinging it. It is also sensitive to rough fish or powerful shrimp that may pick at its polyps.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate, gentle, but variable water flow that encourages full polyp extension without causing them to retract or tear.
  • Water Requirements: Requires extreme stability and cleanliness. The tank must be mature with very low nutrient levels (Nitrate < 5 ppm, Phosphate < 0.05 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Trace elements (especially Iron) are often required. Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Goniopora sp. (Micro Polyp Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Zen Garden Micro Goni
  • Other Common Names: Micro Flower Pot Coral, Polychromatic Goniopora
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8+ inches)
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive, but sensitive to aggression)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Requires frequent, fine-particulate feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established, stable reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but only with passive tankmates and strict spacing from aggressive corals.
  • Care Level: Advanced to Expert

products.title: Swamp Gas Galaxea
products.body_html:
Swamp Gas Galaxea: The Yellow-Green Collector Coral

The Swamp Gas Galaxea (Galaxea fascicularis) is a distinctive and highly fluorescent collector strain of Galaxea coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Galaxea, commonly known as "Star Corals," are easily recognized by their dense, distinct corallites and long, defensive tentacles.

This specific strain is named "Swamp Gas" for its unusual, uniform color palette, which typically features a skeleton base and oral disc in a subtle pale yellow, tan, or light brown. This base is dramatically contrasted by intense, bright neon yellow-green or lime-green tentacles. The overall effect is a strong, gaseous fluorescence, especially under actinic (blue) lighting.

The Swamp Gas Galaxea grows in a massive, encrusting, or hemispherical morphology. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, often growing quickly once established. Like all Galaxea, its aggressive nature requires careful placement to prevent it from damaging nearby corals.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae) but is also a highly capable Carnivore. Its large tentacles actively feed.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and robust polyp extension. The tentacles readily capture food. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions. Feed in the evening when tentacles are fully extended.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Extremely Aggressive. Galaxea is notorious for being one of the most aggressive LPS corals. It extends long, clear sweeper tentacles (which can reach 6+ inches), especially at night, that deliver a potent sting. It is essential to give the Swamp Gas Galaxea a large area of clearance (6+ inches) from all neighboring corals.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Extremely Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate, turbulent water flow to keep the tentacles extended and clean.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-250 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Galaxea fascicularis (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Swamp Gas Galaxea
  • Other Common Names: Lime Green Galaxea, Yellow-Green Star Coral
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dense colonies (12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Extremely Aggressive (Uses long, potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 40 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires extensive spacing (6+ inches).
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Mother Of All Galaxea
products.body_html:
Mother Of All Galaxea: The Polychromatic Spiky Coral

The Mother Of All Galaxea (MOA Galaxea) is an extremely rare and highly prized collector strain of Galaxea coral (Galaxea fascicularis), a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Galaxea, commonly known as "Star Corals," are unique due to their dense colony formation and long, spiky septa that give them a distinctive, aggressive texture.

This specific strain is celebrated for its incredible, multi-layered coloration, which typically includes an icy blue or deep purple base skeleton contrasted by neon green or chartreuse tentacles and, most notably, bright yellow, orange, or reddish tips on the septa and tentacles. The unique combination makes it a spectacular showpiece.

The MOA Galaxea grows in a massive, hemispherical, or encrusting morphology with corallites (polyps) that remain distinct. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, often growing rapidly once established. However, maintaining its maximum color requires moderate light and stable, clean water parameters.

Diet & Compatibility:

Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions. It extends long, clear sweeper tentacles, often several inches long (up to 6 inches or more), especially at night, which possess a powerful sting. It is absolutely crucial to give the MOA Galaxea a large area of clearance (6+ inches) from all neighboring corals.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Extremely Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate, turbulent water flow to keep the tentacles clean and extended.

  • Primary Common Name: Mother Of All Galaxea (MOA Galaxea)

products.title: Sector 001 Favites Brain
products.body_html:
Sector 001 Favites Brain: The Collector's Blue and Green Gem

The Sector 001 Favites Brain is a highly popular and coveted collector strain of Favites coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Favites species are often known as "Brain Corals" or "War Corals" and are prized for their hardiness and dense, honeycomb-like structure.

This specific strain is named for its distinctive, powerful, and contrasting coloration: a dominant base color of deep metallic blue or violet that covers the walls (septa/coenosteum) and is intensely accented by individual corallite mouths (centers) in vibrant neon green or chartreuse. This coloration creates a dramatic, jewel-like appearance, especially under actinic (blue) lighting.

The Sector 001 Favites Brain grows in an encrusting and massive or dome-shaped morphology, with the corallites tightly packed. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, making it a great option for intermediate hobbyists. Its maximum blue and green color saturation requires stable, pristine water and moderate light intensity.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae) but is also a capable Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for promoting dense tissue and vibrant growth. Its mouths are receptive to fine particulate foods, especially when polyps are extended at night. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Aggressive. Favites corals, like many other brain corals, extend long, powerful sweeper tentacles at night to stake their territory. It is crucial to give the Sector 001 Favites at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals to prevent stinging and tissue damage.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate water flow. Avoid directing strong, laminar flow directly onto its fleshy tissue.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Favites sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Sector 001 Favites Brain
  • Other Common Names: Blue Base Green Center Favites, Sector 001 War Coral
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Aggressive (Territorial, uses potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires strict spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Turquoise Rainbow Favia
products.body_html:
Turquoise Rainbow Favia: The Icy Blue and Orange Masterpiece

The Turquoise Rainbow Favia is a visually spectacular and highly prized collector strain of Favia coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Favia species are robust corals commonly known as "Moon Corals" or "Brain Corals."

This specific strain is named for its unique polychromatic coloration, dominated by cool tones. It typically features a base color of bright turquoise, icy blue, or pale lavender along the walls (septa/coenosteum). This cool tone is sharply contrasted by pronounced corallite mouths (centers) in warm neon orange, red, or pink, creating a stunning rainbow effect. The intensity of the blue and turquoise hues is heavily dependent on specific lighting and pristine water quality.

The Turquoise Rainbow Favia grows in an encrusting and massive or dome-shaped morphology, with clearly defined, individually colored corallites. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, but its high value and color demands push its care level toward Moderate to Advanced. It requires stable parameters and moderate light intensity for optimal coloration.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae) but is also a robust Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and maintaining health and vibrant coloration. Its mouths are visible and receptive, especially at night when polyps extend. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Aggressive. Favia corals, like other brain corals, extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night to defend their territory. It is essential to give the Turquoise Rainbow Favia at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals, particularly passive or delicate species.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate water flow. Avoid directing strong, laminar flow directly onto its fleshy tissue.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

  • Scientific Name: Favites sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Jelly Bean Favites
  • Other Common Names: Multicolored War Coral, Festive Favites
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Aggressive (Territorial, uses potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires strict spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Favia of the Gods
products.body_html:
Favia of the Gods: The Divine Multicolored Brain Coral

The Favia of the Gods is an exceptionally rare and highly sought-after collector strain of Favia coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Often referred to as a "Moon Coral" or "Brain Coral," Favia species are prized for their unique coloration and robust, massive growth forms.

This specific strain is named "Favia of the Gods" due to its near-perfect, intense polychromatic coloration, often featuring a base color of metallic blue, violet, or deep purple that contrasts dramatically with neon green or chartreuse centers (corallite mouths) and often incorporates secondary colors like red or pink along the walls (septa/coenosteum). Its vivid colors are highly dependent on clean water and optimal light spectrum.

The Favia of the Gods grows in an encrusting and massive or dome-shaped morphology, with clearly defined individual corallites. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, but its high value and reliance on stable, pristine water to display maximum color saturation push its care level toward Moderate to Advanced.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a robust Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for promoting dense tissue and vibrant color. Its mouths are visible and receptive, especially at night when polyps extend. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Aggressive. Favia corals, like other brain corals, extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night to defend their territory. It is essential to give the Favia of the Gods at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals, particularly passive or delicate species.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate water flow. Avoid strong, direct laminar flow which can irritate the tissue.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Favia sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Favia of the Gods
  • Other Common Names: God Favia, Divine Brain Coral, Polychromatic Favia
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Aggressive (Territorial, uses potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires strict spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate to Advanced

products.title: Blue Skull Favia
products.body_html:
Blue Skull Favia: The Iridescent Blue Brain Coral

The Blue Skull Favia is a highly sought-after collector strain of Favia coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Favia species are often referred to as "Moon Corals" or "Brain Corals" and are foundational pieces in many reef aquariums due to their durability and dense, patterned growth.

This specific strain is named for its mesmerizing, iridescent deep blue or violet base that coats the walls (septa/coenosteum), often contrasted by bright green or neon yellow centers (corallite mouths). The "Skull" name may refer to the recessed, pitted appearance of the corallites against the smooth blue tissue. The blue coloration is highly dependent on blue-spectrum lighting.

The Blue Skull Favia grows in an encrusting and massive or dome-shaped morphology, with individual corallites clearly separated by pronounced walls. It is considered a Hardy LPS coral, making it an excellent choice for intermediate hobbyists. It requires stable water parameters and protection from high light, which can cause its vibrant blue to fade or bleach.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic but is also an effective Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for faster growth and maintaining health. Its mouths are visible and receptive, especially at night. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Aggressive. Favia corals, like many other brain corals, extend long, potent sweeper tentacles at night to defend their territory. It is essential to give the Blue Skull Favia at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals, particularly when placed near passive or delicate species.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate water flow. Avoid excessive flow directly on the tissue.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Favia sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Blue Skull Favia
  • Other Common Names: Blue Favia, Iridescent Brain Coral
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Aggressive (Territorial, uses potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires strict spacing.
  • Care Level: Moderate

products.title: Biohazard Favites
products.body_html:
Biohazard Favites: The High-Contrast Brain Coral

The Biohazard Favites is a highly popular and dramatic collector strain of Favites coral, a type of Large Polyp Stony (LPS) coral. Favites species are often confused with Favia (though they are now often grouped into the broader Merulinidae family) and are commonly known as "Brain Corals" or "War Corals."

This specific strain is named "Biohazard" for its striking, high-contrast, and almost warning-sign-like coloration: a vivid lime green or neon yellow base that is separated by deep red or bright orange walls (septa/coenosteum). The corallites (mouths) are tightly packed, giving it a classic, mesmerizing brain-like or honeycomb appearance.

The Biohazard Favites grows in an encrusting and massive or dome-shaped morphology. It is considered a relatively Hardy LPS coral, making it a great option for intermediate hobbyists or beginners who are diligent with water stability. Its vibrant colors are best displayed under moderate light and stable parameters.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is a Mixotroph, meaning it is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving most energy from symbiotic algae) but is also a capable Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for promoting faster growth, deeper colors, and maintaining overall health. Its mouths are readily visible and receptive. Feed 2-3 times per week with meaty foods, such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, cyclops, or fine coral foods like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions. Feed in the evening when polyps are extended.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe but is Aggressive. Favites corals extend long, powerful sweeper tentacles at night, which can sting and kill nearby corals. It is crucial to give the Biohazard Favites at least 3-4 inches of clear space from all neighboring corals.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, but Aggressive/Territorial toward other corals. It should be positioned on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate, indirect light and moderate water flow. Avoid directing strong, laminar flow directly onto its fleshy tissue.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate 5-10 ppm, Phosphate 0.05-0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-9.5), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1350 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-200 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Favites sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Biohazard Favites
  • Other Common Names: Biohazard War Coral, Brain Coral
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can grow into large, dome-shaped colonies (8-12+ inches)
  • Temperament: Aggressive (Territorial, uses potent sweeper tentacles)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits greatly from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes, but requires strict spacing.
  • Care Level: Beginner to Moderate

products.title: Strawberry Lemonade Cyphastrea
products.body_html:
Strawberry Lemonade Cyphastrea: The Pink and Yellow Contrast

The Strawberry Lemonade Cyphastrea (Cyphastrea species) is a highly unique and visually appealing Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral, celebrated for its sweet, pastel color combination. This specific strain is characterized by a strikingly vibrant pink to reddish-pink base that is densely dotted with contrasting bright lemon yellow or pale green polyps. The coloration provides a beautiful, delicate look that stands out in a mixed reef.

The Strawberry Lemonade Cyphastrea is a vigorous encrusting coral, spreading readily across rockwork in a massive and plating morphology. Its bright colors are best maintained under moderate light intensity and stable, clean water conditions. It offers a unique visual break from the typically deep blues and greens of other corals, thriving particularly well on vertical rock surfaces.

This coral is considered Easy to Moderate in care. It is highly tolerant of fluctuations in flow and is much hardier than most high-end Acropora or delicate Montipora species, making it an excellent candidate for the intermediate hobbyist.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also an opportunistic Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but benefits from active night feeding.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for supporting rapid encrusting growth and maintaining its vibrant color saturation. The polyps are large and receptive to fine particulate foods. Feed 2-3 times per week with fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Cyphastrea species are known for being non-aggressive and do not extend long, powerful sweeper tentacles. However, always ensure aggressive LPS species (like Euphyllia) are not placed close enough to sting its delicate, growing edge.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It thrives best on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate light and moderate, non-turbulent water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-250 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Cyphastrea sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Strawberry Lemonade Cyphastrea
  • Other Common Names: Pink Base Yellow Polyp Cyphastrea, Pastel Cyphastrea
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can encrust indefinitely; forms large, beautiful sheets.
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive option for rockwork)
  • Care Level: Easy to Moderate

products.title: Pumpkin Patch Cyphastrea
products.body_html:
Pumpkin Patch Cyphastrea: The Orange and Green Delight

The Pumpkin Patch Cyphastrea (Cyphastrea species) is a highly attractive and distinctive Small Polyp Stony (SPS) coral, celebrated for its unique coloration that evokes the Halloween season. This specific strain is characterized by a bright, vibrant orange base that is thickly scattered with contrasting neon green or sometimes yellow polyps. The combination of orange and green makes it a standout piece.

The Pumpkin Patch Cyphastrea is an excellent encrusting coral, spreading quickly over rockwork in a massive and plating morphology. Its bright, fluorescent colors are easily maintained under moderate light intensity and stable, clean water conditions. It offers beautiful color and texture, often placed on vertical or shaded surfaces where it can still thrive.

This coral is considered Easy to Moderate in care. It is highly tolerant of varying flow and light conditions and is significantly hardier than more delicate SPS genera, making it a great choice for hobbyists transitioning into hard corals.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also an opportunistic Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but actively feeds, often extending its polyps both day and night.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for supporting rapid growth and vibrant coloration. The polyps are large and receptive to fine particulate foods. Feed 2-3 times per week with fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Cyphastrea species are non-aggressive and do not possess long, potent sweeper tentacles. However, as an encrusting coral, ensure that aggressive LPS species (like Euphyllia or Chalices) are placed far enough away to prevent them from stinging the growing edge.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It thrives best on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate light and moderate, non-turbulent water flow.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-250 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Cyphastrea sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Pumpkin Patch Cyphastrea
  • Other Common Names: Orange Base Green Polyp Cyphastrea, Pumpkin Cyphastrea
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can encrust indefinitely; forms large, plating sheets.
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive option for rockwork)
  • Care Level: Easy to Moderate

products.title: Meteor Shower Cyphastrea
products.body_html:
Meteor Shower Cyphastrea: The Blue and Red Flasher

The Meteor Shower Cyphastrea (Cyphastrea species) is one of the most popular and visually dynamic Small Polyp Stony (SPS) corals, famed for its vivid, high-contrast coloration and relatively easy care requirements. This specific strain is beloved for its deep, vibrant blue or purple base that is thickly dotted with numerous bright, contrasting reddish-orange or reddish-pink polyps, resembling a meteor shower against a night sky.

The Meteor Shower Cyphastrea is an excellent encrusting coral, rapidly covering rockwork and providing deep color to the reef structure. Its growth form is typically massive and encrusting. The brightness of the polyps, which are usually extended, makes this coral fluoresce strongly under actinic (blue) lighting, creating a mesmerizing effect.

This coral is considered Easy to Moderate in care. It is highly adaptable to various lighting and flow conditions and is generally a much more forgiving SPS coral than Acropora or delicate Montipora species, making it an ideal choice for the beginner advancing to hard corals.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic but is also a highly opportunistic Carnivore. It relies on zooxanthellae for most of its energy but actively feeds, often extending its polyps during the day and night.
  • Feeding: Supplemental target feeding is highly beneficial for supporting growth, color vibrancy, and polyp extension. The polyps are large and highly receptive to fine particulate foods. Feed 2-3 times per week with fine foods, such as cyclops, rotifers, or commercial coral powders like Reef Roids and amino acid solutions.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Cyphastrea species are non-aggressive and do not extend long, potent sweeper tentacles. However, as an encrusting coral, ensure aggressive LPS species (like Euphyllia) are not placed close enough to sting its tissue.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Passive toward other corals. It thrives best on mid-to-lower rockwork where it receives moderate light and moderate, non-turbulent water flow. It can tolerate shaded areas better than many other SPS corals.
  • Water Requirements: Requires stable parameters. The tank should be mature with low to moderate nutrient levels (Nitrate < 10 ppm, Phosphate < 0.1 ppm). Critical parameters: Alkalinity (dKH 8.0-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1300-1450 ppm). Demands Moderate PAR lighting (100-250 PAR).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Cyphastrea sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Meteor Shower Cyphastrea
  • Other Common Names: Blue Base Red Polyp Cyphastrea, Meteor Cyphastrea
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Can encrust indefinitely; forms large, plating sheets.
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Passive toward other corals)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 20 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, non-aggressive option for rockwork)
  • Care Level: Easy to Moderate

products.title: Screwdriver Zoa
products.body_html:
Screwdriver Zoa: The Neon Orange and Yellow Cocktail

The Screwdriver Zoa (Zoanthus species) is a vibrant and popular collector strain of Zoanthid (Colonial Anemone/Polyp). Zoanthids are highly regarded for their hardiness, rapid colonization, and their ability to bring intense, saturated colors to the reef tank.

This specific strain is named "Screwdriver" after the classic orange and yellow cocktail. It is characterized by an intense, bright orange or reddish-orange skirt (tentacles) that provides a fiery border. The center oral disk and mouth are typically a sharply contrasting neon yellow or brilliant gold. This high-fluorescence combination of orange and yellow makes the polyps glow vividly under actinic (blue) lighting.

The Screwdriver Zoa grows in a colonial, matting morphology, spreading over rockwork and forming dense clusters. It is considered an Easy care coral. Its robustness and rapid growth rate make it an excellent and forgiving choice for both beginner and seasoned reef hobbyists.

⚠️ CRITICAL SAFETY WARNING: PALYTOXIN ⚠️

Zoanthids, especially certain species, may contain Palytoxin, which can be extremely hazardous if it enters the bloodstream, is ingested, or becomes aerosolized (e.g., by handling damaged polyps, scrubbing rocks, or boiling). Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including gloves and eye protection, when handling Zoanthids. Never use boiling water or tools that may atomize the coral material. Consult a physician immediately if exposed.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also a highly efficient Filter Feeder/Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Zoanthids feed readily and greatly benefit from targeted feeding, which encourages larger polyp size and faster colony growth. Target feed 2-3 times per week with shrimp eggs, rotifers, brine shrimp, or fine coral food powders.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Zoanthids do not actively sting neighbors, but they will aggressively compete for space by growing over other corals. Provide space to prevent overgrowth.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Competitive toward other corals. It should be positioned on low to mid-rockwork where it receives Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR), which helps maximize the fluorescent colors, and moderate, non-turbulent water flow to keep the colony clean and deliver food.
  • Water Requirements:** Requires stable parameters. Zoanthids are tolerant of slightly higher nutrient levels than SPS. Target ranges: Alkalinity (dKH 7.5-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1400 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Zoanthus sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Screwdriver Zoa
  • Other Common Names: Orange Skirt Yellow Mouth Zoa, Citrus Zoa
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Polyps typically 0.5 to 0.75 inches in diameter, forms large mats
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Competitive via overgrowth; potentially toxic)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, durable beginner coral)
  • Care Level: Easy

products.title: Pandora Zoa
products.body_html:
Pandora Zoa: The Electric Blue and Neon Green Target

The Pandora Zoa (Zoanthus species) is one of the most famous, highly fluorescent, and popular collector strains of Zoanthid (Colonial Anemone/Polyp). Zoanthids are universally valued for their hardiness, rapid colonization, and brilliant, saturated colors.

This specific strain is named "Pandora" for its otherworldly, glowing appearance, characterized by a classic, high-contrast "target" pattern. It features an intense, glowing electric blue or teal oral disk (face) that is sharply defined and ringed around a brilliant, neon green or yellow mouth/center. The skirt (tentacles) often has a darker rim or purple hue, focusing all visual attention on the vibrant, fluorescent blue and green center.

The Pandora Zoa grows in a colonial, matting morphology, spreading rapidly over rockwork and forming dense clusters. It is considered an Easy care coral and is highly durable, making it an excellent, robust choice for any established reef tank.

⚠️ CRITICAL SAFETY WARNING: PALYTOXIN ⚠️

Zoanthids, especially certain species, may contain Palytoxin, which can be extremely hazardous if it enters the bloodstream, is ingested, or becomes aerosolized (e.g., by handling damaged polyps, scrubbing rocks, or boiling). Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including gloves and eye protection, when handling Zoanthids. Never use boiling water or tools that may atomize the coral material. Consult a physician immediately if exposed.

  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Zoanthids do not actively sting neighbors, but they will aggressively compete for space by growing over other corals. Provide space to prevent overgrowth.

It should be positioned on low to mid-rockwork where it receives Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR), which is ideal for maximizing the fluorescent colors, and moderate, non-turbulent water flow to keep the colony clean and deliver food.
  • Water Requirements:** Requires stable parameters. Zoanthids are tolerant of slightly higher nutrient levels than SPS. Target ranges: Alkalinity (dKH 7.5-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1400 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Zoanthus sp. (Specific Strain)

products.title: Halle Berry Zoa
products.body_html:

products.title: Fruit Loops Zoa
products.body_html:
Fruit Loops Zoa: The Multi-Colored Colonial Polyp

The Fruit Loops Zoa (Zoanthus species) is an iconic and highly collectible strain of Zoanthid (Colonial Anemone/Polyp). Zoanthids are celebrated for their ease of care, resilience, and complex, polychromatic patterns.

This specific strain is named "Fruit Loops" due to its resemblance to the brightly colored cereal rings. It is characterized by a complex, multi-color profile that can include a neon green or blue center mouth, surrounded by rings or bands of color on the oral disk that transition through red, hot pink, orange, and yellow. The combination creates a highly saturated, multi-color target appearance on each polyp, making it one of the most sought-after Zoas.

The Fruit Loops Zoa grows in a colonial, matting morphology, spreading over rockwork and forming dense clusters. It is considered an Easy care coral and is highly durable, making it an excellent choice for beginner and advanced reefers alike.

⚠️ CRITICAL SAFETY WARNING: PALYTOXIN ⚠️

Zoanthids, especially certain species, may contain Palytoxin, which can be extremely hazardous if it enters the bloodstream, is ingested, or becomes aerosolized (e.g., by handling damaged polyps, scrubbing rocks, or boiling). Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including gloves and eye protection, when handling Zoanthids. Never use boiling water or tools that may atomize the coral material. Consult a physician immediately if exposed.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also a highly efficient Filter Feeder/Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Zoanthids feed readily and greatly benefit from targeted feeding, which encourages larger polyp size and faster colony growth. Target feed 2-3 times per week with shrimp eggs, rotifers, brine shrimp, or fine coral food powders.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Zoanthids do not actively sting neighbors, but they will aggressively compete for space by growing over other corals. Provide space to prevent overgrowth.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Competitive toward other corals. It should be positioned on low to mid-rockwork where it receives Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR), which is sufficient for growth and color, and moderate, non-turbulent water flow to keep the colony clean and deliver food.
  • Water Requirements:** Requires stable parameters. Zoanthids are tolerant of slightly higher nutrient levels than SPS. Target ranges: Alkalinity (dKH 7.5-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1400 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Zoanthus sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Fruit Loops Zoa
  • Other Common Names: Polychromatic Zoa, Rainbow Zoanthid
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific)
  • Max Size: Polyps typically 0.5 to 1 inch in diameter, forms large mats
  • Temperament: Peaceful (Competitive via overgrowth; potentially toxic)
  • Diet: Photosynthetic and Carnivore (Benefits from target feeding)
  • Minimum Tank Size: 10 Gallons (Established reef)
  • Reef Compatibility: Yes (Excellent, durable beginner coral)
  • Care Level: Easy

products.title: Florida Orange Zoa
products.body_html:
Florida Orange Zoa: The Highly Fluorescent Orange Polyp

The Florida Orange Zoa (Zoanthus species) is an extremely popular and vibrant collector strain of Zoanthid (Colonial Anemone/Polyp). Zoanthids are highly favored for their ease of care, rapid growth, and stunning, saturated colors.

This specific strain is named for its intense, unmistakable coloration. It is characterized by a highly fluorescent, brilliant orange skirt (tentacles) that contrasts sharply with a bright yellow or gold mouth and center disk. The polyps exhibit a stunning, saturated glow that stands out vividly under blue-spectrum reef lighting.

The Florida Orange Zoa grows in a colonial, matting morphology, spreading over rockwork and forming dense clusters. It is considered an Easy care coral and is highly durable, making it an excellent choice for beginner and advanced reefers alike.

⚠️ CRITICAL SAFETY WARNING: PALYTOXIN ⚠️

Zoanthids, especially certain species, may contain Palytoxin, which can be extremely hazardous if it enters the bloodstream, is ingested, or becomes aerosolized (e.g., by handling damaged polyps, scrubbing rocks, or boiling). Always wear appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE), including gloves and eye protection, when handling Zoanthids. Never use boiling water or tools that may atomize the coral material. Consult a physician immediately if exposed.

Diet & Compatibility:

  • Diet: This coral is primarily Photosynthetic (deriving energy from symbiotic algae, zooxanthellae) but is also a highly efficient Filter Feeder/Carnivore.
  • Feeding: Zoanthids feed readily and greatly benefit from targeted feeding, which encourages larger polyp size and faster colony growth. Target feed 2-3 times per week with shrimp eggs, rotifers, brine shrimp, or fine coral food powders.
  • Reef Compatibility: It is Reef Safe and generally Passive. Zoanthids do not actively sting neighbors, but they will aggressively compete for space by growing over other corals. Provide space to prevent overgrowth.
  • Temperament: Peaceful toward fish and motile invertebrates, and Competitive toward other corals. It should be positioned on low to mid-rockwork where it receives Moderate PAR lighting (75-150 PAR), which is sufficient for growth and color, and moderate, non-turbulent water flow to keep the colony clean and deliver food.
  • Water Requirements:** Requires stable parameters. Zoanthids are tolerant of slightly higher nutrient levels than SPS. Target ranges: Alkalinity (dKH 7.5-10.0), Calcium (400-450 ppm), and Magnesium (1250-1400 ppm).

Quick Facts

  • Scientific Name: Zoanthus sp. (Specific Strain)
  • Primary Common Name: Florida Orange Zoa
  • Other Common Names: Gold Mouth Zoa, NEON Orange Zoanthid
  • Origin: Cultured/Maricultured (Originals from the Indo-Pacific/Florida region)
  • Max Size: Polyps typically 0.5 to 1 inch in diameter, forms large mats
